title: Scott's Conjecture
statement_text: For every graph $H$, the class of graphs which do not contain any subdivision of $H$ as an induced subgraph is $\chi$-bounded.
context_text: Scott (1997) conjectured this topological generalisation of the Gyárfás–Sumner conjecture, proving it for trees and for $K_4$. The conjecture was disproved in full generality by Pawlik et al. (2014), but determining which specific graphs $H$ satisfy it remains an active area of research.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture holds when $H = P_{a,b}$, i.e., the complete bipartite graph $K_{a,b+2}$ together with an additional vertex adjacent to exactly two vertices on the larger side of the bipartition, for all $a,b \geq 1$; as a special case this covers $H = K_4^{++}$, the graph obtained from $K_4$ by subdividing two disjoint edges.
paper_title: A positive instance of Scott's Conjecture on induced subdivisions
